assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Section 2.4 shows all 7 versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) marked **NO** in the Affected? column. Each version ships serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is above the fix threshold of 1.0.135. The summary states: 'No supported version is affected by CVE-2026-28940.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 8 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md heading reads 'Decision: Case C -- Close as Not a Bug (Not Affected)'. Section '2. Proposed Status Transition' states: 'Transition TC-8002 to Closed with resolution Not a Bug'. Section 'No Remediation Tasks Created' explicitly states: 'Because no supported versions are affected (Case C), no remediation tasks are created.'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md Section '1. Proposed Comment on TC-8002' includes a table listing all 7 versions with their serde_json versions and 'Not affected' status: 2.1.0 (1.0.137), 2.1.1 (1.0.137), 2.2.0 (1.0.138), 2.2.1 (1.0.138), 2.2.2 (1.0.138), 2.2.3 (1.0.139), 2.2.4 (1.0.139). The comment text reads: 'No supported versions ship a vulnerable version of serde_json. Version impact analysis confirms all supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is outside the affected range (< 1.0.135).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 8 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md Section '3. Proposed VEX Justification' states the VEX Justification custom field (customfield_12345) is configured and proposes the value 'Component not Present' with explanation: 'the vulnerable version of serde_json (< 1.0.135) is not shipped in any supported product version.' The proposed API call shows: jira.edit_issue("TC-8002", fields={"customfield_12345": "Component not Present", ...})

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 8 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md Section 'No Remediation Tasks Created' explicitly states: 'Because no supported versions are affected (Case C), no remediation tasks are created. There is no upstream backport task, no downstream propagation task, and no system package remediation task. The issue is simply closed with the evidence documented above.' No remediation.md file exists in the outputs directory (only data-extraction.md, version-impact.md, and triage-outcome.md were produced).
